summary: Light front-end bid from a US auction with run-and-drive notes. We targeted a lot with isolated sheet metal damage to keep repair scope predictable for Ukraine partners. Ocean leg to an EU hub plus land delivery to Ukraine; customs shown here are illustrative and can change based on vehicle specs, auction outcome, and applicable taxes at import.
verdict: Best for repair and resale in Ukraine with a trusted local body shop.
client_label: Illustrative example — not a single transaction

summary: Three-row SUV example for a buyer prioritizing space over cosmetic perfection. Rear quarter damage and controlled repair scope illustrate how salvage plus repair can land below comparable retail in the destination market, but duties and taxes remain estimate-based until the exact import scenario is confirmed.
verdict: Strong for families comfortable with documented repair and certification steps.
client_label: Illustrative example — not a single transaction

summary: Illustrative clean-title EV export scenario from East Coast inventory to Jebel Ali. UAE customs duty often starts at 5%, but VAT, port fees, and local compliance can change the actual final landed figure depending on the buyer structure and current rules.
verdict: Clean-title export example for UAE with an efficient Jebel Ali route.
client_label: Illustrative example — not a single transaction

summary: Illustrative hail-damage work-truck calculation showing how cosmetic repair can compare against local retail in Europe. Import costs, duties, and repair budgets depend on the exact truck spec, route, and destination-side processing at the time of shipment.
verdict: Good for buyers accepting cosmetic repair trade-offs in exchange for lower acquisition cost.
